skill.name: github-project-contributor-finder-api-skill
skill.description: Extract GitHub repository details and contributor contact information using the BrowserAct API. Search by keywords, star count, and update dates to discover projects and find active developers.
skill.summary: Find GitHub repositories and extract contributor profiles via API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 78 potential issues, but evaluation found nearly all to be false positives. The 41 external_commands detections were markdown backticks in SKILL.md documentation, not actual shell execution. The critical heuristic combined these false positives with legitimate network and env_access patterns. Network requests are documented API calls to the legitimate BrowserAct service. Environment variable access uses os.getenv() for secure API key retrieval, which is best practice. No malicious intent or dangerous patterns were found.

low_findings.title: External API Network Requests
low_findings.description: The script makes HTTP POST and GET requests to api.browseract.com, a third-party service. This is the documented and intended behavior for the BrowserAct GitHub scraping API integration. All endpoints use HTTPS with standard timeouts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests are clearly documented API calls to BrowserAct. URLs are hardcoded to the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint and use HTTPS.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable API Key Access
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables using os.getenv(). This is the secure and recommended method for handling API credentials. No secrets are hardcoded in the source code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct use of os.getenv for API key retrieval follows security best practices. No hardcoded secrets are present in the code.

content.value_statement: Manually searching GitHub for contributors and project details is time-consuming. This skill automates discovery by connecting to the BrowserAct API to extract repository metrics and contributor contact information.
actual_capabilities: Search GitHub repositories by keywords with star count and date filters
Extract repository details including owner, description, and star count
Find contributor profiles with bios and social links
Collect personal websites and Twitter handles of developers
Paginate through search results to gather multiple repositories
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key to function
Subject to BrowserAct concurrent task limits
Dependent on BrowserAct API availability and response times

use_cases.title: Tech recruiting pipeline
use_cases.description: Recruiters gather contributor profiles and contact links from popular repositories to build candidate pipelines for open positions.

use_cases.title: Open-source project discovery
use_cases.description: Developers search for trending repositories by keywords and star count to discover valuable tools and libraries in their domain.

use_cases.title: Developer outreach campaigns
use_cases.description: Marketers collect personal websites and social handles of active contributors to run targeted outreach for developer tools and services.

prompt_templates.title: Search repositories by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Find GitHub repositories related to machine learning with at least 500 stars updated since 2025-01-01

prompt_templates.title: Find contributors with filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for browser automation repositories with 100+ stars and get the top 10 contributors for each

prompt_templates.title: Build a lead list
prompt_templates.prompt: Find React framework repositories with 1000+ stars, then extract all contributor profiles with personal websites and Twitter handles for developer outreach

prompt_templates.title: Competitor analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for repositories matching competitor keywords, analyze their top contributors, and compile a report of active developers with their social profiles

output_examples.input: Find Python web framework repositories with 500+ stars
output_examples.output: Repository: FastAPI (12,000 stars) - Owner: tiangolo - Description: FastAPI framework, high performance... Contributors: tiangolo (github.com/tiangolo, fastapi.tiangolo.com, @tiangolo)

output_examples.input: Search for machine learning repositories updated in 2025
output_examples.output: Found 3 repositories: transformers (Hugging Face, 120K stars), llama.cpp (ggerganov, 65K stars), langchain (langchain-ai, 95K stars). Contributors include personal websites and Twitter handles.

output_examples.input: Get contributors from trending DevOps tools
output_examples.output: Repository: kubernetes (Kubernetes project, 110K stars). Top contributors: brendandburns (github.com/brendandburns), lavalamp (github.com/lavalamp). Personal websites and bios included where available.

best_practices: Always verify your BrowserAct API key is set before running the skill
Use specific keywords and reasonable star thresholds to get relevant results
Respect rate limits and concurrent task restrictions to avoid service interruptions
anti_patterns: Do not use overly broad keywords that return thousands of irrelevant repositories
Avoid requesting excessive page turns that may hit API limits or take too long
Do not share your BrowserAct API key in prompts or conversation history

faq.question: What API key do I need?
faq.answer: You need a BrowserAct API Key. Visit https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations to get one and set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: How long does execution take?
faq.answer: Execution typically takes several minutes because the skill uses automated browser operations. The script outputs timestamped status logs so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: What data does the skill return?
faq.answer: The skill returns repository details like name, URL, owner, description, and star count. For contributors, it returns username, profile URL, bio, other repositories, personal website, and Twitter handle.

faq.question: Can I filter by star count and update date?
faq.answer: Yes. You can specify minimum stars and a date filter in YYYY-MM-DD format. Only repositories meeting both criteria will be returned.

faq.question: What happens if the task fails?
faq.answer: The skill has built-in retry logic for transient failures. If the error is an invalid API key or concurrent task limit, it will guide you to resolve the issue instead of retrying.

faq.question: Is this skill compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code agents. The agent will execute the Python script and handle the API interaction automatically.
